## Summary

# Codebase Audit

## Stance

**Artifacts are claims, not evidence — verify against primary sources before reporting.**

An audit reads a workspace claiming "feature X NOT DONE" and reports that as a finding without checking if feature X actually exists in the code. This is the core failure mode. Search the codebase before concluding.

---

## When to Use

| User says... | Dimension |
|-------------|-----------|
| "Security vulnerabilities" | security |
| "Performance bottlenecks" | performance |
| "Test coverage" | tests |
| "God objects" / "tight coupling" | architecture |
| "ROADMAP drift" / "template drift" | organizational |
| "Accessibility" / "a11y" / "WCAG" | accessibility |
| "Quick health check" | quick |

---

## Core Workflow

1. **Create investigation file** — `kb create investigation "audit/dimension-description" --model <model-name>` (or `--orphan`)
2. **Pattern search** — Run dimension-specific search commands
3. **Evidence collection** — Concrete examples with file:line references
4. **Analysis** — Root cause, severity assessment, ROI prioritization
5. **Documentation** — Investigation file with findings + baseline metrics

**Track progress:** `bd comments add <beads-id> "Phase: [phase] - [details]"`

---

## Evidence Hierarchy

| Source Type | Examples | Treatment |
|-------------|----------|-----------|
| **Primary** (authoritative) | Actual code, test output, observed behavior | This IS the evidence |
| **Secondary** (claims to verify) | Workspaces, investigations, decisions | Hypotheses to test |

---

## Model Awareness (Probe vs Investigation Routing)

Check SPAWN_CONTEXT.md for `### Models (synthesized understanding)` section.

**If model markers present** → Your findings likely confirm/contradict/extend a model. Create a probe:
- `.kb/models/{model-name}/probes/{date}-{slug}.md`
- Focus on: how findings relate to model's invariants

**If absent** → Standard investigation file.

---

## Investigation File Setup

```bash
kb create investigation "audit/dimension-audit-description" --model <model-name>  # or --orphan
```

After creating:
1. Fill Question field with specific audit focus
2. Document findings progressively (not at end)
3. Set Resolution-Status when completing (Resolved/Recurring/Synthesized/Mitigated)

---

## Dimensions

### Security

**Focus:** Secrets exposure, injection vulnerabilities, auth/authz gaps, cryptography, dependencies, OWASP Top 10

**Key searches:**
```bash
rg "password|secret|api_key|token|private_key" --type py --type js -i
rg "execute\(.*%|\.format\(|f\".*FROM|f\".*WHERE" --type py
rg "subprocess\.call|os\.system|eval\(|exec\(" --type py
rg "innerHTML|dangerouslySetInnerHTML|\.html\(" --type js
rg "password\s*=\s*['\"]|api_key\s*=\s*['\"]" --type py --type js
```

**Output:** Findings rated Critical/High/Medium/Low with remediation steps.

---

### Performance

**Focus:** Algorithmic complexity, N+1 queries, resource usage, I/O, caching, concurrency

**Key searches:**
```bash
rg "for.*:\s*\n.*for.*:" --type py -U          # Nested loops (O(n²))
find . -name "*.py" -o -name "*.js" | xargs wc -l | sort -rn | head -20
rg "TODO.*performance|FIXME.*slow|HACK.*optimize" -i
```

**Output:** Findings with profiling data and optimization recommendations.

---

### Tests

**Focus:** Coverage gaps, flaky tests, missing test types, test quality, organization

**Key searches:**
```bash
comm -23 <(find . -name "*.py" | grep -v test | sort) \
         <(find . -name "test_*.py" | sed 's/test_//' | sort)
rg "sleep|time\.sleep|random\.|datetime\.now" tests/
rg "def test_" tests/ -l | xargs rg "assert" -L     # Tests without assertions
rg "Mock|patch|MagicMock" tests/ -c | sort -rn | head -10
```

**Output:** Testing gaps with risk assessment and coverage metrics.

---

### Architecture

**Focus:** God objects, tight coupling, missing abstractions, circular dependencies, SOLID violations

**Key searches:**
```bash
rg "^\s+def \w+\(self" --type py | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10  # God classes
find . -name "*.py" -o -name "*.js" | xargs wc -l | sort -rn | head -20
rg "^\s{16,}(if|for|while|def)" --type py                          # Deep nesting
```

**Output:** Architectural issues with refactoring effort estimates.

---

### Organizational

**Focus:** ROADMAP drift, documentation drift, template drift, state duplication, manual sync points

**Process (4 phases, 90-150 min):**
1. Pattern search — automated drift detection
2. Evidence collection — validate patterns, gather concrete examples
3. System amnesia analysis — identify violated coherence principles
4. Documentation — investigation with recommendations

**Key searches:**
```bash
git log --oneline --since="30 days ago" | rg "feat:|fix:" | head -20
rg "remember to|don't forget|make sure to update" docs/ --type md -i
rg "TODO.*update|FIXME.*sync" --type py --type md -C 2
```

**Root cause categories:** Return trip tax, context switching, no single source of truth, manual sync points, no observability.

**Detailed workflow:** See [reference/dimension-organizational-detail.md](reference/dimension-organizational-detail.md)

---

### Accessibility

**Focus:** Semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, color/contrast, forms, dynamic content, media

**Key searches:**
```bash
rg "onClick|on:click" --glob "*.{svelte,jsx,tsx}" -C 1 | rg "<(div|span|li)"
rg "<img " --glob "*.{svelte,jsx,tsx,html}" | rg -v "alt="
rg "<input|<select|<textarea" --glob "*.{svelte,jsx,tsx,html}" -C 2 | rg -v "label|aria-label"
rg "tabIndex=\"[1-9]|tabindex=\"[1-9]" --glob "*.{svelte,jsx,tsx,html}"
```

**Severity:** Critical (blocks AT users) → High (major a11y gap) → Medium (degraded) → Low (minor)

**Anti-pattern:** Adding `role="button"` to a `<div>` instead of using `<button>`. Recommend semantic HTML first, ARIA only when native elements can't express the semantics.

**Detailed workflow + WCAG mapping:** See [reference/dimension-accessibility-detail.md](reference/dimension-accessibility-detail.md)

---

### Quick

**Process (30-60 min):** Automated scan across all categories → Triage to top 10 by ROI → Document findings.

**Output:** Top 10 findings sorted by ROI with recommended next steps (which focused audit to run).

**When to use:** Monthly health monitoring, before major work, onboarding, deciding which focused audit to run.

**When NOT to use:** Known problem area (use focused audit), need deep analysis.

**Detailed scan commands + templates:** See [reference/dimension-quick-detail.md](reference/dimension-quick-detail.md)

---

## Parallel Mode

For comprehensive multi-dimension audits: spawn 5 parallel Haiku agents (one per dimension), synthesize results.

**When to use:** Full health check needed, time-constrained, comprehensive coverage.

**Detailed workflow:** See [reference/parallel-mode.md](reference/parallel-mode.md)

---

## Available Dimensions Summary

| Dimension | Time | Output |
|-----------|------|--------|
| security | 30-90 min | Security findings with severity + remediation |
| performance | 30-90 min | Bottlenecks with profiling + optimization recs |
| tests | 30-90 min | Coverage gaps with risk assessment |
| architecture | 30-90 min | Structural issues with refactoring estimates |
| organizational | 90-150 min | Drift patterns with system amnesia analysis |
| accessibility | 60-90 min | WCAG-mapped findings with remediation |
| quick | 30-60 min | Top 10 across all categories |

---

## Anti-Patterns

| Anti-Pattern | Fix |
|-------------|-----|
| Vague findings ("has drift issues") | Concrete evidence: file:line, commit sha, count |
| No reproducibility | Document all search commands + baseline metrics |
| Recommendations too vague ("fix drift") | Specific: "Add auto-update to orch complete" |
| No prioritization | ROI framework: impact vs effort matrix |
| Reporting artifact claims as findings | Verify against primary sources (actual code) |
| "Add ARIA" without context | Specify file:line, what label should convey, which WCAG criterion |

---

## Completion

**Deliverables:**
- Investigation file with findings, evidence, recommendations
- Pattern search commands documented (reproducibility)
- Baseline metrics captured for re-audit
- Recommendations prioritized by ROI

**Report:** `bd comments add <beads-id> "Phase: Complete - Audit findings: [count], Recommendations: [count]"`

**Discovered work:** Create beads issues for high-priority findings:
```bash
bd create "SECURITY: description" --type bug -l triage:ready     # Critical/High
bd create "ARCHITECTURE: description" --type task -l triage:review  # Medium/Low
```
